introduction

Source: vignettes/introduction.Rmd
introduction.Rmd
library(BayesRTMB)
#> Loading required package: RTMB

BayesRTMB Package Reference Manual

1. Model Definition and Construction

Dim(dim = 1, type = NULL, lower = NULL, upper = NULL, random = FALSE)

Specifies the dimensions, type, bounds, and random effects of a parameter.

  • Arguments:
    • dim: Parameter dimensions (e.g., scalar=1, vector=length, matrix=c(rows, columns)).
    • type: Parameter type (e.g., "vector", "matrix", "ordered", "simplex", "corr_matrix", "cov_matrix", "CF_corr"). If omitted, it is automatically inferred from the dimensions.
    • lower: Lower bound of the parameter.
    • upper: Upper bound of the parameter.
    • random: If TRUE, treats this parameter as a random effect, making it subject to Laplace approximation (applicable to both MAP estimation and MCMC).

rtmb_model(data, code, par_names = list(), init = NULL, view = NULL, null_target = NULL)

Combines data and model definition blocks to build an R6 class model object.

  • Arguments:
    • data: Named list containing observation data and constants.
    • code: Model definition block generated by rtmb_code().
    • par_names: List of specific names corresponding to the dimensions of each parameter (optional).
    • init: List or numeric vector of initial values. If omitted, values are automatically generated randomly.
    • view: Character vector of parameter names to display preferentially in summary() outputs.
    • null_target: Used to construct a null model simultaneously; specify the target parameter to fix and the prior distribution to disable (e.g., "delta ~ cauchy(0, r)").

rtmb_code(...)

The block defining the model structure. Passed to the code argument of rtmb_model.

  • Block Types:
    • data: (Optional) Block declaring variables explicitly passed from the data argument.
    • parameters: Block declaring model parameters using the Dim function.
    • model: Block defining the likelihood and prior distributions.
    • transform: (Optional) Block defining parameters transformed from the main parameters.
    • generate: (Optional) Block defining generated quantities (e.g., predictions, log-likelihoods for WAIC) calculated from the posterior distribution.

2. Estimation Methods (Methods of RTMB_Model)

$optimize(...)

Performs Maximum A Posteriori (MAP) estimation or Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE). * Arguments: * num_estimate: Number of times to run the estimation to avoid local optima. * laplace: If TRUE, marginalizes random effects using Laplace approximation. * optimizer: Specifies the optimizer ("nlminb" or "optim"). * method: Optimization method when using optim (e.g., "BFGS"). * map: Used to fix parameters. Generally manipulated automatically via $null_model().

$sample(...)

Draws posterior samples using the No-U-Turn Sampler (NUTS). * Arguments: * sampling: Number of sampling iterations per chain. * warmup: Number of warmup (burn-in) iterations per chain. * chains: Number of Markov chains. * thin: Thinning interval. * delta: Target acceptance rate for NUTS (default: 0.8). * max_treedepth: Maximum tree depth for NUTS (default: 10). * parallel: If TRUE, runs chains in parallel using the future package. * laplace: If TRUE, performs NUTS sampling on the marginalized posterior using Laplace approximation.

$variational(...)

Performs Automatic Differentiation Variational Inference (ADVI). * Arguments: * iter: Number of optimization iterations. * tol_rel_obj: Convergence threshold (relative change in ELBO). * num_samples: Number of posterior samples to generate from the fitted variational distribution. * num_estimate: Number of times to run ADVI (treated as chains to avoid local optima). * alpha: Learning rate for the optimizer. * laplace: If TRUE, applies ADVI to the marginalized posterior using Laplace approximation.

3. Other Methods (Methods of RTMB_Model)

$null_model(target, value = 0)

Creates a new model object with the specified parameter fixed to value (useful for calculating Bayes Factors). target can be specified as a string like "delta" or "delta ~ cauchy(0, 1)".

$print_code()

Prints the internal model structure (parameter list and log_prob function code) to the console.

4. Fit Result Objects

MAP_Fit Class

Stores the results of $optimize(). * Fields: par (parameter list on the constrained scale), objective (negative log-posterior), log_ml (log marginal likelihood via Laplace approximation), df_fixed / random_effects (summary data frames of estimates). * Methods: * $summary(max_rows = 20, digits = 2): Outputs a table summarizing estimates, standard errors, and 95% Wald confidence intervals.

MCMC_Fit Class

Stores the results of $sample() and performs post-processing of posterior samples. * Methods: * $summary(pars = NULL, ...): Returns a data frame of summary statistics including Rhat, effective sample size (ESS), posterior mean, and credible intervals. * $draws(pars = NULL, chains = NULL, inc_random = FALSE, inc_transform = TRUE, inc_generate = TRUE): Extracts posterior samples as a 3D array (iteration, chain, variable). * $bridgesampling(method = "warp3", seed = NULL): Estimates the marginal likelihood via Bridge Sampling. * $generated_quantities(code): Computes generated quantities (GQ) for the extracted posterior samples and stores them in the object.

VB_Fit Class

Stores the results of $variational(). * Methods: * $summary(pars = NULL, ...): Returns a data frame of summary statistics (Note: Rhat and ESS are not computed). * $draws(pars = NULL, best_only = TRUE, ...): Extracts posterior samples. If best_only = TRUE, extracts samples only from the run with the highest ELBO. * $plot_elbo(tail_n = 1000): Plots the Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO) history during optimization. * $plot_trajectory(pars = NULL): Plots the parameter trajectory in the final optimization window. * $generated_quantities(code): Computes generated quantities.

On this page
